grouped_hits.title: Post-discharge Malaria Chemoprevention(PMC) Study
matched_sentences: This study is a confirmatory efficacy trial in Kenya and Uganda to determine the efficacy and safety of malaria chemoprevention post-discharge.

In Malawi, 3 months of post-discharge malaria chemoprevention with monthly 3-day treatment courses of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) in children with severe malarial anemia prevented 31% of deaths and readmissions.

We hypothesize that an additional three months of malaria chemoprevention with monthly 3-day treatment courses with DHA-piperaquine (each providing about 4 weeks of post-treatment prophylaxis) provided during the post-discharge period to children recently admitted with severe anemia is superior to reduce all-cause readmission and mortality rates by 6 months compared with 2 weeks of post-treatment prophylaxis provided by the single course of oral AL when given as part of the standard in-hospital care around the time of discharge.
